entry.title: pls share the t code for update invoice no and date

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migration – Why and Getting Ready for Migration
entry.content:
Jocelyn,

Do you recommend creating Business Catalogs in CONF scope or CUST? We only have one-client systems. 

Is there an advantage between CONF and CUST scopes for Business Catalogs, if there is only one client. Even if we had multiple clients, we'd want the same Launchpad configuration in all clients. We are on S/4HANA 2020 FPS02.

Thanks

entry.title: Gender field (custom) in Quick Apply to Late Stage
entry.content:
Hello- we are currently working on quick apply. One of the issues we are having is when you initially apply as a US candidate, you are required to answer the knockout questions and self-identify EEO/Disability/Veteran questions.

When they move onto late-stage application, the only field that doesn’t auto-populate over with their selection is Gender. What we learned form our support partner is since this field is not a SF standard field ‘Male’ or ‘Female’, and it is custom, it will not populate over. They confirmed they have other customers who have this behavior as well and a KBA confirmed this as well for us.

If you use Quick Apply, what is the behavior on your end when candidate is in late stage (if you are using a custom field)? Is it requiring Gender to be answered? We are hoping to have fewer manual entries for the candidate and wanted to see if there's a workaround without having to change from customer to standard field.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: How to stop HTML Viewer from refreshing?
entry.content: Are you owning the code where the HTML viewer is used? If so, you shouldn't create a new instance of the HTML control in GUI each roundtrip. Instead the instance of the Cl_GUI_HTML_VIEWER should be a global variable in the program and only be instantiated at the very beginning.

entry.title: SAP Build Code - Create Application Logic with Joule
entry.content:
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-code-test-drive.html

"In the Storyboard, click on the Purchases entity under Services, and select Open in Graphical Modeler".

entry.title: Re: Call transaction FB02 to 3rd screen
entry.content: Call the transactuion via ABAP4_CALL_TRANSACTION. Wrap this call in a RFC-Call starting new task.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

I'm Christopher from South Africa.

I am holder of Bcom in Law and I'm really interested in this initiative.

Regards, 

Christopher

entry.title: Re: Warning - Upgrading individual databases from v9.3 PL...
entry.content:
Hello Pipalia1,

SAP Note #2128874 - Upgrade Wizard Precheck: Database structure check for SAP Business One indexes explains the mechanism of the index checks during upgrade.

It should not give warnings because you didn't modify any table index.

One thing I'm suspecting is that you upgraded from v9.3 PL12 to v10 SP2402. The overview note of SP2402 https://me.sap.com/notes/3413880 asks the following path.

You can upgrade to SAP Business One 10.0 SP 2402 directly from the following versions:

  • SAP Business One 10.0 PL00-PL02
  • SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2008 - SP 2311

If you are running an SAP Business One 9.2 or 9.3 release version, we recommend that you first upgrade to SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2208 and then to 10.0 SP 2402.

the upgrade wizard of 10.0 SP2402 might not take v9.3 PL12 into consideration during upgrade, therefore regarded unexpected index deviation as user altered change.

 

Regards

EunSeok

entry.title: SD - Passing Freight Line of $0.00 in tax call
entry.content:
Hello,

Currently, we're facing an issue where our sales documents that are free shipping do not pass a freight line in the tax call.  This only became an issue with the Colorado Retail Delivery Fee.  Normally, not passing a $0.00 wouldn't have been an issue, however now our Tax Engine doesn't return the $0.27 fee because there is no shipping indicator passed in the call.   When I test out adding a freight condition at $0.00 it just isn't included in the call.  Can passing a freight condition at $0.00, be determined in the Sales and Distro configuration?

entry.title: Re: EWM Change GR posting date to back date
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I am also interested in changing the GR date in EWM to a date in the past.

Is there still no possibility to do that as we are in higher EWM release versions in the meantime?

BR

entry.title: Re: Not able to open solman_setup tcode
entry.content:
Hello @DM2K 

Check below SAP Note (symptom #2) 

2452098 - solman_setup points to wrong URL after a system copy - SAP for Me

2503418 - SOLMAN_SETUP URL loading with blank screen - SAP Solution Manager - SAP for Me

I hope these Notes would help in resolving your issue.

Regards,

Vignesh

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030
entry.content:
As per my understanding the compliance check report only checks whether the IDoc message types are used together with the standard function modules.

Therefore it would be sufficient to simply copy the standard modules to custom modules and continue to use the standard IDoc types (WMTORD, WMCATO, etc).

Do you all agree?

Best regards

Nathalie

Leo.

entry.title: Re: Edit in the MIGO table?
entry.content:
Hello,

I did no say he should edit the table. I gave some advise how to use the transaction. By table he ment

the table in the transaction. And MB1C is in S/4 Hana not supported any more, because of that you have to use MIGO

(I liked MB1C more).

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP GTM-Error occured in Profit Simulation
entry.content: Hello Team, Can anyone help me to get this fixed please?

entry.title: How to create specific entity region cache in SAP Commerce
entry.content:
Introduction

Entity Region cache is a critical region for SAP Commerce performance.  In this article, we will how we can create dedicated region cache for some types to release pressure on Entity Region cache.

 

Why New Cache Region

Entity region cache is capturing by default all types (Items instances). In most case, this strategy is working well when product catalogue is not too large and when user traffic is limited.

However, we can see very high instance eviction when we reach this cache size limit. It is impacting badly performance when eviction is on low volatility data as Product, Category, and Features.

In other hand, high volatility data should be stored in dedicated cache to not pollute default generic region cache (entity region cache). Often, we can identify Cart, CartEntry, PromotionResult, PromotionAction as high volatility data.

By inspecting cache in HAC, we can see easily this behavior.

 

How to create a new Cache Region

Let imagine, we want to create a dedicate cache for Cart and CartEntry type. We need first to get type code. It can be found through BackOffice in Type search

Then, we can declare in spring XML our new cache region

 

<!-- new cache region dedicated for Cart -->
<bean name="cartCacheRegion" class="de.hybris.platform.regioncache.region.impl.EHCacheRegion" lazy-init="true">
<constructor-arg name="name" value="cartCacheRegion" />
<constructor-arg name="maxEntries" value="1000" />
<constructor-arg name="evictionPolicy" value="LRU" />
<constructor-arg name="statsEnabled" value="true" />
<constructor-arg name="exclusiveComputation" value="false" />
<property name="handledTypes">
<array>
<value>43</value> <!-- Cart -->
<value>44</value> <!-- CartEntry -->
</array>
</property>
</bean>

<!-- Add cache region in HAC view -->
<bean id="cartCacheRegionRegistrar" class="de.hybris.platform.regioncache.region.CacheRegionRegistrar" c:region-ref="cartCacheRegion" />

 

This spring XML must be part of global context (project.properties)

 

# Cache regions need to go to the global context
custom.global-context=custom-cache-spring.xml

 

 

After this cache region creation, we see that Cart/CartEntry are not polluting anymore our generic Entity Region cache where low volatility data can stay for long time.

 

Conclusion

Create a new dedicated region cache for some types, it is quite simple and straightforward when we know how to do.

For more advanced use case, you should not hesitate to ask SAP Expert Services help.

entry.title: Re: Taking huge heap memory by SAP JCO library
entry.content:
Not sure, what you mean by "IDoc file", because if you use JCo, the IDoc is not transferred as a file, but as an internal table. The memory consumption of this table can be calculated easily: the table is of type EDI_DD40, which has 7 fields with a total length of 1063 characters. So in a Unicode system, the memory consumption is 2126 bytes per line. (Roughly speaking, one line in the table corresponds to one segment in the IDoc.)

Next we need to know, that the data is transferred via tRFC protocol, which means that JCo needs to have two copies of the data in memory: first for the DATA table containing the recorded function call (ARFC_DEST_SHIP function module), and then for the SDATA table (type EDI_DD40) of the IDOC_INBOUND_ASYNCHRONOUS function module. The required memory for the DATA table is not easy to calculate, because it contains partially compressed data, which can be processed in a kind of "streaming mode", but I guess, if we just take a factor of two, we are on the safe side.

So a good rule of thump to calculate the size requirement of an IDoc in JCo/Java is:
4k x number of segments

If that 2790 MB char[][] corresponds to one SDATA table of one IDoc, this would mean that this IDoc (package) had around 1.4 million segments?! Can this be true?? I don't think that any single IDoc can be that big, so more likely, you have defined the partner profile outbound parameter for this IDoc to "collect" several IDocs into one package? In that case, you should make the package size smaller. (See transaction WE20: in the detail view of the Partner Profile corresponding to the Receiver & IDoc type, there is a field named "Pack. Size". You could experiment a bit with it and cut it down, until the IDoc packages reach a "manageable" size in terms of total number of segments.)

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Welcome @LeoCortes - great that you are interested in the SAP Community and researching and further learning! I can give you some initial link suggestions below:

  • Products and Technology boards, for example Enterprise Resource Planning
  • Topic pages (get to know about SAP products), for example SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Managed Tags (subscribe to your topic areas of interest)
  • Join the Career Corner, and see what articles and job opportunities are being posted

entry.title: 2024 - Patches/fixes for SAP MII
entry.content:
03/19/2024:

XMII 15.5 SP 000 Patch 36:

  • Conditional | Added Conditional Action Block log entries in transaction debug output, note 3440246
  • History | Improved History Mode for the Scheduler Service, note 3442938
  • Query Number format is not working as expected, note 3443976
  • Adding Transaction Persistence as parameter in case of transaction called via SOAPRunner interface, note 3445523

03/14/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.3 SP 001 Patch 24:

  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689

XMII 15.3 SP 001 Patch 53:

  • DB Object is closed error is thrown in case of sql transaction, note 3364376
  • Unable to read DataBufferActionInstance parameter using databuffer service, note 3395994
  • Usage Status Monitoring for DataServer with wrong date, note 3429629
  • Workbench file export generates the wrong cemd file for SSCE Dashboards, note 3430755
  • Additional logs for transaction action failure due to timeoutException, note 3407507
  • MII transaction fails with SAP DBTech JDBC: Object is closed error, note 3416130

03/11/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.5 SP 001 Patch 7:

  • System Management - Projects screen assigned roles editing Issue, note 3294426
  • Details header not changing after selecting any tag in simulator services under data services, note 3264633
  • Open activities with checked-out files are still visible after disabling and enabling the NWDI configuration, note 3433017
  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689

XMII 15.5 SP 001 Patch 12:

  • Enhancing the logs for MII Start and Stop services, note 3438776
  • Conditional | Added Conditional Action Block log entries in transaction debug output, note 3440246
  • Details header not changing after selecting any tag in simulator services under data services, note 3264633
  • Version history missing in MII workbench, note 3418076
  • Additional logs and traces in message processing module of MII, note 3426125
  • Usage Status Monitoring for DataServer with wrong date, note 3429629
  • NWDI version history displays limited number of activities, note 3432814
  • Open activities with checked-out files are still visible after disabling and enabling the NWDI configuration, note 3433017
  • DependencyManager | Added dependency check for the MII_ADMIN component version, note 3308632
  • Additional Logging in XMII-Module, note 3340019
  • System Warning window on MII home page for component incompatibility, note 3399037

03/07/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.4 SP 001 Patch 18:

  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689

XMII 15.4 SP 001 Patch 45:

  • Conditional | Added Conditional Action Block log entries in transaction debug output, note 3440246
  • DB Object is closed error is thrown in case of sql transaction, note 3364376
  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689
  • MII transaction fails with SAP DBTech JDBC: Object is closed error, note 3416130

03/05/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.5 SP 000 Patch 20:

  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689

XMII 15.5 SP 000 Patch 35:

  • DB Object is closed error is thrown in case of sql transaction, note 3364376
  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689
  • JMS updates causing scheduler breakdown, note 3360558
  • Usage Status Monitoring for DataServer with wrong date, note 3429629
  • Workbench file export generates the wrong cemd file for SSCE Dashboards, note 3430755
  • Additional logs for transaction action failure due to timeoutException, note 3407507
  • MII transaction fails with SAP DBTech JDBC: Object is closed error, note 3416130

02/16/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.4 SP 001 Patch 17:

  • NWDI configuration gives null pointer exception on save, note 3342267

XMII 15.4 SP 001 Patch 44:

  • Support for X-CSRF-Token in MII HTTP POST action block, note 3429523
  • Usage Status Monitoring for DataServer with wrong date, note 3429629
  • Workbench file export generates the wrong cemd file for SSCE Dashboards, note 3430755
  • SSO cookie logged and displayed in MII Log Viewer, note 3410041
  • Get Jar Signing related information which gets checked while opening workbench., note 3346027
  • NWDI configuration gives null pointer exception on save, note 3342267
  • Scheduler history cleanup default value changed from 24 hours to 72 hours, note 3394218
  • Unable to read DataBufferActionInstance parameter using databuffer service, note 3395994
  • Scheduler history comes blank when job is stuck or not executed in last hour, note 3396099
  • JMS updates causing scheduler breakdown, note 3360558

02/09/2024:

XMII 15.5 SP 001 Patch 11:

  • Running remote workbench with proxy for jar based workbench, note 3369660
  • Unable to read DataBufferActionInstance parameter using databuffer service, note 3395994
  • Source table is missing inside the Global setting on SSCE, note 3424542
  • Settings.view | Event Parameters table headers not visible, note 3426391
  • MII PortalContent.zip file not found under Tools and Archives, note 3427393
  • i5Grid getRowCount() error, note 3428910
  • UIElementTextFieldM | Text Field UI Element size, note 3429413
  • Workbench file export generates the wrong cemd file for SSCE Dashboards, note 3430755
  • Additional logs for transaction action failure due to timeoutException, note 3407507

02/06/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.3 SP 001 Patch 23:

  • NWDI configuration gives null pointer exception on save, note 3342267

XMII 15.3 SP 001 Patch 52:

  • SSO cookie logged and displayed in MII Log Viewer, note 3410041
  • Cannot view changelist details or process a changelist, note 3336846
  • Get Jar Signing related information which gets checked while opening workbench., note 3346027
  • MII workbench login with x.509 certificate based SSO login, note 3370246
  • NWDI configuration gives null pointer exception on save, note 3342267
  • Running remote workbench with proxy for jar based workbench, note 3369660
  • Scheduler history cleanup default value changed from 24 hours to 72 hours, note 3394218
  • New Workbench launcher can not open the workbench if server url has '-' in it, note 3394368
  • Scheduler history comes blank when job is stuck or not executed in last hour, note 3396099
  • JMS updates causing scheduler breakdown, note 3360558

01/17/2024:

XMII 15.4 SP 001 Patch 43:

  • Cannot view changelist details or process a changelist, note 3336846
  • Catalog query with production events is returning Non-PE when there are records in PE table, note 3338596
  • Duplicate Rows inserted in PE table due to duplicate shift rows in MPM_TC37T table, note 3345988
  • PIC Analysis Query - Production events displaying incorrect data, note 3219251
  • Duplicate rows inserted in production table when PE job is run again, note 3333504
  • i5Chart (Gauge Chart Type) is starting from zero value for any change in Value., note 3404399

01/12/2024:

XMII 15.5 SP 001 Patch 10:

  • Catalog query with production events is returning Non-PE when there are records in PE table, note 3338596
  • Additional Logging in XMII-Module, note 3340019
  • MII workbench login with x.509 certificate based SSO login, note 3370246
  • Duplicate Rows inserted in PE table due to duplicate shift rows in MPM_TC37T table, note 3345988
  • Scheduler history cleanup default value changed from 24 hours to 72 hours, note 3394218
  • Production events job creates duplicate records in Production Events table, note 3300655
  • Duplicate rows inserted in production table when PE job is run again, note 3333504
  • i5Chart (Gauge Chart Type) is starting from zero value for any change in Value., note 3404399
  • SSO cookie logged and displayed in MII Log Viewer, note 3410041
  • Bug in SSCE text field i5ValueHelp, note 3413146
  • MII transaction fails with SAP DBTech JDBC: Object is closed error, note 3416130

entry.title: What are the use cases of SAP Datasphere over SAP BW4/HANA
entry.content:
ABSTRACT 

This blog explores the significance of SAP Datasphere as a centralized hub for managing data in organizations, aiming to address challenges related to scalability, outdated analytical capabilities, data governance, security, and compliance. Through two distinct use cases, the blog demonstrates how SAP Datasphere integrates with DataRobot for advanced machine learning capabilities and enables business builders to identify potential clients, understand their needs, and develop tailored strategies. By leveraging self-service functionalities, data virtualization, and partnerships with platforms like DataRobot, SAP Datasphere empowers businesses to drive innovation, improve decision-making, and foster growth in the data and analytics space. 

 

Introduction to why datasphere? 

SAP Datasphere is the hub for all our data. We are connected to various source systems and store the data into the datasphere. We have a very decentralized business, so the goal of implementing the datasphere was to centralize to a single source of truth. 

SAP Datasphere offers several benefits for business users that may make it a more attractive option than SAP BW: 

  1. Self-service functionalities: SAP Datasphere provides self-service functionalities for business users, enabling them to build their own models and reports using Business Builder, which is designed for semantic modeling. This allows business users to be more involved in data modeling, which can help to relieve the IT department and implement requirements more quickly. 
  2. Separation of responsibilities: SAP Datasphere enables a strict separation of responsibilities between IT and the business departments. IT can focus on technical issues such as data cleansing, preparation, business logics, high availability, performance, and data access control, while the business departments can model based on the prepared data models and enrich and supplement the data models with CSV uploads. 
  3. SAP BW Bridge: SAP BW Bridge is an extension of SAP Datasphere that enables the migration of data flows from on- premises systems to the cloud. This allows data modeling in SAP Datasphere to take place with data from the SAP BW system, without the need for a full SAP BW/4HANA system. 
  4. Hybrid data modeling: SAP Datasphere's hybrid data modeling capabilities allow businesses to federate data via SAP BW/4HANA Model Transfer or use SAP BW Bridge to import data models based on analytical queries. This enables businesses to leverage their existing data models and enrich them using self-service functionalities. 
  5. Data virtualization:  SAP Datasphere's data virtualization enables the integration of data from various sources, such as SAP and non-SAP systems, without physically moving or replicating the data.  

 

SCENARIOS 

First use case of SAP Datasphere with Data robot: 

The customer, a large retail organization, has been using SAP BW for several years. However, they are now facing several challenges that are limiting their ability to innovate and grow. These challenges include: 

Limited scalability: The customer's SAP BW system is struggling to handle the increasing volume of data being generated by their business operations. This is causing delays in processing and reporting, impacting on the customer's ability to make timely decisions. 

Outdated analytical capabilities: The customer's SAP BW system is not equipped with advanced analytical capabilities, such as machine learning and natural language processing. This is limiting the customer's ability to unlock new insights from their data and drive innovation in their business operations. 

Data governance challenges: The customer is facing challenges in managing and governing their data across various systems. This is causing inconsistencies in data quality and increasing the risk of errors in their business operations. 

Security and compliance challenges: The customer is struggling to maintain trust with their stakeholders due to security and compliance challenges in their SAP BW system. This is impacting their ability to attract new customers and partners. 

To address these challenges, the customer has decided to migrate from their SAP BW system to SAP Datasphere. This migration will enable the customer to leverage advanced analytical capabilities, data governance, and scalability, driving innovation and growth in their business operations. 

The migration plan will involve the following steps: 

  • Assess the current SAP BW system 
  • Plan the migration 
  • Migrate the data models: Using SAP BW Bridge, migrate the data models from the SAP BW system to SAP Datasphere. This may involve converting the data models to a format compatible with SAP Datasphere, such as the SAP Data Warehouse Cloud (SDWC) model. 
  • Migrate the data quality rules: Migrate the data quality rules from the SAP BW system to SAP Datasphere. This may involve converting the rules to a format compatible with SAP Datasphere, such as the SAP Data Quality (SDQ) rule format. 
  • Migrate the data transformation processes: Migrate the data transformation processes from the SAP BW system to SAP Datasphere. This may involve converting the processes to a format compatible with SAP Datasphere, such as the SAP Data Integration (SDI) process format. 
  • Test the migration 
  • Deploy the migrated data platform 
  • Monitor and maintain the migrated data platform 

To use tables from SAP Datasphere in DataRobot, you can follow these steps: 

  • Connect to SAP HANA using DataRobot's JDBC connector. 
  • In the DataRobot platform, navigate to the AI Catalog tab. 
  • Click "Add to Catalog" and select "Existing Data Connection". 
  • Choose the connection that holds the data you want to add and select an account. 
  • Enter the credentials for the connection, and once validated, select a source for the data. 
  • After the content is selected, click "Proceed with registration". 
  • DataRobot registers the new tables (datasets) and you can then create projects from them or perform other operations, like sharing and querying with SQL. 

By migrating from their SAP BW system to SAP Datasphere, the customer will be able to overcome the challenges they are currently facing and drive innovation and growth in their business operations. DataSphere focuses on data integration, governance, and advanced analytics, while DataRobot specializes in automated machine learning and model deployment.  

Figure 1: model transfer and data transferring from sap BW system to sap datasphere using sap BW bridge and datasphere connectors 

Figure 2: integrated architecture of sap datasphere and datarobot  

 

Second use case of SAP Datasphere with Business builder functionality: 

The business builder in SAP Datasphere is responsible for identifying potential clients, understanding their business needs, and developing strategies to meet those needs. The business builder can leverage Collibra, a knowledge management platform, to enhance their capabilities in this process. Here is a step-by-step breakdown of the use case: 

Identify potential clients: The business builder identifies potential clients by conducting market research, attending industry events, and networking with professionals in the data and analytics space. 

Understand client business needs: The business builder gains insights into the client's business needs by conducting interviews, analyzing client data, and observing client behavior. 

Develop strategies to meet client needs: The business builder develops strategies to meet the client's business needs by offering tailored real-time data and analytics solutions, providing consulting services, or offering training and support services. 

Prepare sales pitches: The business builder prepares sales pitches that highlight the benefits of SAP Datasphere and the strategies they have developed to meet the client's business needs. These pitches are tailored to the specific needs of each client. 

Engage in sales activities: The business builder engages in sales activities, such as presenting sales pitches, conducting demonstrations, and participating in industry events. These activities are targeted at potential clients and are designed to generate interest in SAP Datasphere and the strategies the business builder has developed. 

Follow up and close deals: After engaging in sales activities, the business builder follows up with potential clients to ensure that their needs are being met. Once the client's needs are being met, the business builder closes the deal and secures the client as a customer for SAP Datasphere. 

Figure 3: Virtualization 

By following these steps, the business builder in SAP Datasphere can effectively identify potential clients, understand their business needs, and develop strategies to meet those needs. This will enable the organization to attract new clients and grow its business in the data and analytics space. 

 

 

Conclusion 

SAP Datasphere, with its advanced self-service capabilities and data virtualization feature, offers a powerful solution for business users to access and analyze their data without relying on IT. By seamlessly integrating with DataRobot, SAP Datasphere enables enterprises to leverage advanced machine learning models, resulting in more intelligent business predictions with advanced analytics. Partnership with DataRobot and integration with Business Builder functionality provides a powerful and comprehensive solution for businesses, enabling them to leverage machine learning models, cut across data silos, and improve their decision-making process.

entry.title: Re: Nodes from dmee do not appear in xml file
entry.content: Compare with a standard such as PAYM / CGI_XML_CT (Your Z name implies a local version specific to your systems)

entry.title: Re: How to stop HTML Viewer from refreshing?

entry.title: Re: How to stop HTML Viewer from refreshing?
entry.content: sdfsdf

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP CF Audit log viewer query
entry.content:
Hi @TasSh,

I don't think there is a way to configure the web view of such logs.

However, you should be able to download the logs files somehow. If not on the web view itself, you can request SAP support team for the full logs files whenever needed. As far as I remember, the log viewer has a download button on the right-hand side of the UI (a down-arrow icon with no text) - At least it is like so for the regular application logs - which I don't recall being different for audit logs.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 with Blazor
entry.content: We have set up and used a couple of components, but we are not actively maintaining them... here is a little playground: https://blazorui5.ceo.do/

entry.title: Re: Recon accounts- Open item managed
entry.content:
Hello,

Please I have a question, I'm preparing the Financial accounting certification and I didn't find an exact answer to this question, 

  • When is it possible to set the open item management indicator in a general ledger account? ( 2 correct answers)
    ● When an account is set up as a balance sheet account
    ● When an account is set up as a secondary cost element
    ● When an account is set up as a reconciliation accounting
    ● When an account has a zero balance
     
    As I know, the Open item management is not used for the reconciliation accounting, is it true?
    Can you please help

entry.title: Limitations Of SAP BW Bridge
entry.content:
ABSTRACT 

In this blog post, we'll delve into the limitations of SAP BW Bridge, a tool used for data replication and provisioning in SAP environments. We'll explore each limitation in detail and discuss alternative solutions to overcome these challenges. 

1. No GUI Support:

Description: SAP BW Bridge lacks support for the traditional SAP GUI interface, posing challenges for managing and maintaining the SAP BW System.

Alternative Solution: Migrate BW objects like infocubes to BW4HANA and then to ADSO of type Datamart before using BW Bridge.

2. Write Interface Only:

Description: Certain data sources like flat files, DB/UD Connect, web service, and HANA sources cannot be loaded through BW Bridge.

Alternative Solutions: Utilize File Interface, SLT, Web Service Interface, and HANA Smart Data Integration for loading data from unsupported sources.

3. No Support for OLAP Engine:

Description: BW Bridge lacks support for OLAP features like hierarchies, navigational attributes, and BW queries.

Alternative Solution: Leverage OLAP functionalities available in SAP Datasphere for data analysis and reporting.

4. Data Tiering Optimization:

Description: SAP BW Bridge has limited storage space compared to on-premises systems, affecting data tiering optimization.

Alternative Solution: Implement real-time replication processes and use SAP Datasphere for data storage and analytics.

5. Mix Modelling Not Possible:

Description: BW Bridge does not support mixed modeling, focusing on data consolidation rather than statistical analyses.

Alternative Solution: Utilize specialized tools like SAP Statistical Analysis Software (SAS) or R for mixed modeling.

6. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Requirement for Planning:

Description: SAC is required for fulfilling planning requirements, potentially impacting existing processes and leading to additional costs.

Alternative Solution: Transition to SAC for integrated planning or explore other planning solutions.

7. Add-On Limitations:

Description: Add-ons like SAP BW/4 HANA Integration Suite and SEM-BCS are not supported in BW Bridge.

Alternative Solution: Consider alternatives like SAP Group Reporting instead of unsupported add-ons.

8. No Nearline Storage (NLS):

Description: BW Bridge lacks support for Nearline Storage, posing challenges for managing infrequently accessed data.

Alternative Solution: Implement separate Nearline Storage solutions like SAP HANA Nearline Storage or Data Services Nearline Storage.

9. Lack of Streaming Process Chains Capability:

Description: Absence of streaming process chains in BW Bridge affects real-time reporting capabilities.

Alternative Solution: Implement streaming solutions like SAP HANA Streaming Analytics to complement BW Bridge for real-time reporting.

10. No Temporal Joins:

Description: Temporal joins cannot be performed in composite provider within BW Bridge.

Alternative Solution: Explore temporal join solutions like SAP HANA Spatial or Data Services Spatial.

11. No DTP Variable Option Available:

Description: DTP lacks support for variables in selection within BW Bridge.

Alternative Solution: Utilize alternative methods for managing variables in DTP selection.

Conclusion:

Understanding the limitations of SAP BW Bridge is crucial for effectively managing data replication and provisioning in SAP environments. By exploring alternative solutions and workarounds, organizations can overcome these challenges and optimize their data management processes.

entry.title: Re: Error during oData API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV in SAP Integ...
entry.content:
Hi @Simon,

Try using a json2xml converter at the start of the iflow - if your sender's adapter is marked as OData as well.

Maybe the OData API doesn't require you to send any JSON payload at all, but from the iFlow perspective data should be assigned to the data model defined on an EDMX. 

Also, import the data model via EDMX file you downloaded from API Business Accelerator Hub. Then perform any mappings necessary in the payload.

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/importing-from-odata

Read more on OData integration here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE/50c996852b32456c96d3161a95544cdb/73bb18f0ccfc4b4c9222106814e29cae.html

There is a nice blog using EDMX to consume OData service here:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-cloud-platform-integration-cpi-part-8-working-with-request-reply/ba-p/13427622

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Assign a user to the cell in SAP SAC
entry.content: In my project we have a dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAP SAC), where we are looking for such functionality, that someone could click on a cell and add another user's login e.g.@ilona2 and type their comment/question there. In our solution we can't use the cell comments and the dimension comment is already in use. We would like to have a similar functionality as in Google sheets, where in any cell someone can type another person's login with "@" sign and that person would be automatically notified and know directly where is the issue in the data.

entry.title: Integration possibilities from SAP CI(Integration Suite) to SWIFT???
entry.content:
Hi All,

While i was exploring the integration possibilities from CI to SWIFT had few questions around this as below.

1. If the integration has to be from S4HANA then there is a direct connector available for SWIFT which we can start configuring from SPRO tcode without the need of CI . Is is the best approach comparing to the other available options???

2. If the connector is not available might be S4HANA release issue or for normal ECC system then that case the traditional way of generating the file in AL11 from where CI will pick the drop onto the target system with all sort of security parameters(encryption/signature) maintained. We dont have standard prepackaged content like what we had for SWIFT SWCV in PI/PO??? Also the SFTP 3.0 which was used in PI/PO for SWIFT integration with the module configurations. How it can be accompanied in CI??? Are there any technical glitches???

3. Any other mechanisms for this integrations???

Please do share your thoughts on this.

Thank You.

Regards,

Farooq

entry.title: Re: Service Layer Load Balancer not behaving as expected...
entry.content:
Hello again,

The intended single end point is SL load balancer.

You need to install only load balancer members in the 2nd server.

the load balancer in the 1st server is the only load balancer in the landcape and serve as a single end point.

 

Regards

EunSeok

entry.title: HANA installation failed
entry.content:
HANA installation failed with below error

/usr/sap/HXE/HDB90/exe/hdbnsutil: error while loading shared libraries: libaio.so.1: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory

entry.title: Is it possible to configure the min and max memory allocation property for the Apache server?
entry.content: It is my understanding that Service Layer is using Apache server in the background. I am unable to find performance optimization and tuning settings for setting min and max memory for the Apache server. Is this configurable for each node or at a server level at all? If so, has anyone found a link to a tech note or some documentation on this at all?

entry.title: Re: Service Layer Load Balancer not behaving as expected...
entry.content:
That's what we were hoping for, but in the SLD, we are seeing two different end points and a service listed for both end points.

We saw an entry in the Control Center for the SLD for the following:

  • VM1:50000; VM1:40000/ServiceLayerController (where the SLD is)
  • VM2:50000; VM2:40000/ServiceLayerController (additional server where only Service Layer was installed as described in Admin guide of the documentation)

So it was unclear what the developers are meant to call when a second load balancing server is installed, because it's not clear from documentation that the server will automatically load balance and we are meant to ignore the second end point.

entry.title: Re: SAP GTM-Error occured in Profit Simulation
entry.content:
Hello Vijay,


There seems to be some customizating missing for Profit Simulation. Have you checked and completed customization Logistics General > Global Trade > Profit Simulation steps?

It must be something about Financial Document Number.

You can also check out SAP Note 585478 - Consulting note for Profit Simulation in GTM and Global Trade Management - From Business Suite to S/4HANA in which there are some SAP Notes need to be implemented. BTW which S/4HANA versions are you using?

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Re: S4 Public Cloud Development : "Logon to the ABAP serv...
entry.content:
Hi SreekanthKrishn, 

I recommend checking Eclipse or ADT for updates here: SAP Development Tools for Eclipse - Latest Software Repository (ondemand.com).

Reinstalling Eclipse may also help. I believe this guide (Install Eclipse for ABAP Development – Discovering ABAP) could help you with that.

BR, Serhii.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: I am Harikrishna Madathala , born in chennai,India, but now am in New Jersey , United States of America for the past 13 years.I work at Microsoft,USA and have 19 years of experience in SAP. I'm driven by curiosity and thrive on challenges, constantly seeking to learn and grow. Creativity is my fuel, and I find joy in exploring new ideas and perspectives. With a passion for innovation, I strive to make a positive impact in all that I do. In every endeavor, I aim to inspire others to embrace their uniqueness and pursue their dreams.

To do this, the objects must be in a package of a global SWC. Is there a way to move all objects to the global package or changing the SWC ZLOCAL of my local package? Assigning the local package to a global structure package (via Superpackage) does not work.

Thanks and Regards, Sebastian

entry.title: SAP Security audit logs
entry.content:
Followed the below steps to get audit logs 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/56bf1265a92e4b4d9a72448c579887af/c769bcb7f36611d3a6510000e835363f.html?q=SM19

but the logs are not in structured format. It has no line break, previous version has 200 characters as limit.
Do we have any option to change the log format?

Or Any other methods available SYSLOG or CSV 

Expected log Structure 
2AU520130409010803000505200009D9a234ba.pDOKUSTAR SAPMSSY1 0201R&0 h020co.pt.com

Analysis and Recommended Settings of the Security ... - SAP Community

Current logs 
2AU520130409010803000505200009D9a234ba.pDOKUSTAR0201R&0h020co.pt.com2AU520130409010803000505200009D90201R&0h020co.pt.com

entry.title: Re: Technical user to access API of CAP application on BTP
entry.content:
Hi @Moritz__,

Thanks for letting me know you can apply updates on the xsuaa instance via CLI. I remember that this was not possible and currently the cokpit application does allow you to do this but it has always failed on me while trying to update an instance of xsuaa.

I am not sure I quite understand the question about debug or dummy viewer. 

Are you asking how you are able to "protect" the Service Key information? 

If so, the service key is protected the same way all other development artifacts are in the space. If you are a Developer of that space, you will be able to create and view it. If you are an Auditor or a Debugger, then you are able to do less stuff on BTP. 

BTP Roles are explained here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/about-roles-in-cloud-foundry-environment

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: there is a simple way to make a Single API POST to create Multiple Rows?
entry.content:
Solved this with the IF Flow function sending 1 line and deleting that line from my List of Object and checking if the list of object is empty and sending another line until the object gets empty.

 

I think this is temporary until i get more experience or some expert tip with a smarter way to do this.

entry.title: Re: pyRFC license and cross license
entry.content:
Not sure whether it helps, but the license terms for our RFC Connectors (to which the "NW RFC Library" belongs, which is used internally by pyRFC) are listed here:

Connectors (sap.com)

entry.title: Unable to maintain entries for custom table in SAP using SM30, after adding a new field to the table
entry.content:
Hello,

There is already a custom table in my development system. And I have add a new field to that table and activated it.
So, I wanted to update already existing entries of this table using SM30. But I am not able to see the new field added on the maintenance screen.


Eg: already have a customtable 'z_xyz' with one field say, 'abc'. values for the field 'abc' is maintained using SM30.
Now, I have added a new field 'def' and activated table in SE11.
Now, I have tried to maintain the table z_xyz using SM30, but not able to see the new field added to the maintenance screen (I could see only the field 'abc' not both 'abc' and 'def')

could anyone help me on this case, so that I can update existing record with the values for the new field created in that table?

entry.title: Re: Unable to maintain entries for custom table in SAP us...
entry.content:
Hi @J_K,

You should go to More > Utilities > Table Maintenance Generator after adding the fields in the table and re-generate the maintenance screens.

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Marketplace Updates not working
entry.content:
Hello all,

I have 5 updates to the marketplace to perform - Delete record, Get record, Replace record, and Create record.

When I do the updates my external API calls to Xano fail.  Specifically, I rely on returning error codes from Xano to provide tailored alerts to my users.  (In Xano I use "Preconditions" with error messages that I return via the rawError message).

If I try creating a user that already exists, without the updates I get:

{"code":"serverError","status":404,"message":"JSON error response from server: {\"code\":\"ERROR_CODE_NOT_FOUND\",\"message\":\"already exists\",\"payload\":\"already exists\"}.","rawError":{"code":"ERROR_CODE_NOT_FOUND","message":"already exists","payload":"already exists"}}

But with the marketplace updates I only get: {"code":"requestFailed","status":404} which I cannot parse to get my "already exists" message.

Also, I actually only had 4 updates to make.  It says I have 5 updates to make.   After making all 4 updates it says I still have 1 to make.  When I try "Updating All", it shows I have no more updates to make, however, my login page gets wiped out.

Any help would be greatly appreciated

entry.title: SAP IBP - Job launch suggestion
entry.content:
Hello, greetings from Ecuador!

I would like to make a suggestion, please consider adding the feature "Notification after finishing job" vía a registered SMS or vía email.

SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain 

 

Thank you!

Gerardo.

entry.title: Re: router.GetRoute("xxx").attachMatched not picking up s...
entry.content:
Hi @guychapman,

My understanding is that div_id and uer_id is not a route, but a parameter to a specific route called contacts.

Therefore, it makes sense it is only being picked up for that particular route.

Once a route is matched (_onRouteMatched), you can then pick up the attributes by name using the 'getParameter(byName)'

Please see the following sample on the documentation:

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/f96d2522a5ca4382a274ae3c6d002ca0

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: Call transaction FB02 to 3rd screen
entry.content:
Find the item number with a small SELECT statement and build a small BDC to go to third screen in FB02, in SHDB recording you could use Edit, Display from item, so first item will be required one. Then use a CALL TRANSACTION with OPTIONS FROM opt 

NB: Alternative : use FB09 and not FB02

entry.title: In a multi-app CAP project, how can I trigger navigation after custom action?
entry.content:
We have a SAP CAP project with multiple applications. Our folder structure looks similar to this:

-app1:

  • webapp:
    • Component.js
    • annotations.cds
    • manifest.js ...

-app2:

  • webapp:
    • Component.js
    • annotations.cds
    • manifest.js ...

I have a custom action defined in the service.js that is triggered when pressing a button in app1:

 this.on(['createContract'], REUnits.drafts, async (req, res) => {
            const idUnit = req.params[0].ID;
            const unitData = await SELECT.from(REUnits).where({ ID: idUnit });
            const aServices = await SELECT.from(UnitServicesNode).where({ ID: idUnit });

            const maxContractCodeResult = await SELECT.one('MAX(contractCode)').from(REContract);
            let maxContractCode = maxContractCodeResult["max ( contractCode )"] || 0;

            // Increment the maximum contract code value by 1
            maxContractCode++;
            const newContract = {
                contractCode: maxContractCode.toString()                
            };

            await INSERT.into(REContract).entries(newContract);

        }); 
I want to trigger navigation to an external app that displays the newly created contract. I used semantic object to carry out navigation succesfully but I don't know how to achieve it after a custom action.

entry.title: Re: A problem occurred while checking the system health (NO_RESPONSE)
entry.content: I'm running same issue - where exactly did you change that key registry?

entry.title: Re: SAP Security audit logs
entry.content:
The answer for your question is on the very same document where you got the expected file format:

If you want an external tool to be able to read the log events, the best option is to store them on the DB.

entry.title: Re: "Failed to Load Metadata" Error in Create KPI --> Entity Set
entry.content:
Hello,
We are also facing same Issue and below are screenshots from maint service t-code where we are able to see metadata.

And below is the error in Fiori while trying to create the ALP:

And below is our CDS view:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZSQL_KPI'

@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true

@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Ex CDS for KPI'

 

@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION

@OData.publish: true

 

define view ZKPI_TILE as select from ekko as Poh

inner join ekpo as poi on Poh.ebeln = poi.ebeln

 

{

key Poh.ebeln as ponumber,

poi.werks as plant,

Poh.bukrs as companycode,

poi.mtart as MaterialType,

poi.matkl as MaterialGrp,

@DefaultAggregation: #SUM

poi.netwr as Value

}

OData , SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA ,

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: Yes I am planning to write blogs and answer questions on SAP HA / SAP installation etc

entry.title: Access Request role search restriction using GRAC_ROLP and Rolename
entry.content:
Hi All, We have a business requirement where we are trying to restrict the roles a requestor can select while submitting an access request. In order to achieve this I'm trying to restrict the role selection using the auth object GRAC_ROLEP and Role name. In BRM we have Business roles named as ***** DENMAK , *** SWEDEN. I'm trying to use a string in the Role name however I'm getting the error- Unsuitable generic input for authorization changed. I came across sap note -3195139 regarding the error. Could someone assist me to know how can I achieve this?

Many Thanks,

Regards,Nandita

entry.title: Early Stock Simulation for PMR documents Posting- Consumption & Consumption Reversal
entry.content:
New functionality:

From 1909, SAP has delivered a remarkable feature which allows early S/4 stock check during consumption or consumption reversal posting from EWM related to PMR documents. This early stock simulation for PMR document posting helps in significantly reducing the number of failed queues, improves the scalability and less user intervention to correct the queues.

This functionality can be activated from below configuration.

Extended Warehouse Management-->ERP Integration-->Goods Movements-->Activate Synchronous Goods Movement Posting

F1 help clearly stating the feature, however, functionality is hidden and less explored by customers. 

Replication Step:

I-S/4 and EWM stock not in sync for some reason. ERP stock check.

EWM stock= 415 PC

S/4 Stock= 73 PC

  II - Create PMR & Stage to PSA.

III- Post consumption by Production using Fiori or NWBC

 EWM has already 415 PCS, however S/4 has 73 PCS ONLY

Post consumption:

Stock simulation check does early stock check in S/4 as well during posting from EWM

PMR qty= 300 PCS, S/4 Stock=73, Variance= 300-73=  227 Shortage

This feature helps in reducing stuck queue in S/4 due to stock deficit.

This functionality works for Post Reversal from Production as well in Embedded 1909 onwards. This feature is not supported in Decentralized EWM on S/4 HANA due to system boundary.

This feature is also not supported during Consumption Posting in RF. No early stock check is called from RF framework. If you try to post from RF, EWM allows however, Queue gets stuck in SMQ2 stating deficit of Stock.

Try to post consumption from RF. NO simulation check is called as compared to Fiori.

System allowed to post consumption from RF and there is no S/4 Early stock simulation called as Fiori and its a missing link.

 Thanks for reading. All test have been performed in S/4 HANA Embedded 2022 Sandbox system

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: @HARIKRISHNAM great. Keep me posted!

entry.title: Re: Ledger Setup in S/4HANA Public Cloud for country India
entry.content:
Review help.sap.com documentation on Ledgers

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6b39bd1d0e5e4099a5b65d835c29c696/8a4fd7531a4d424de10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

 

See Ledger Scenarios: Choosing the Right Combination for Your Business Needs also https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/60e06e02b32c43f0adcc38c540c384d0.html?locale=en-US 

as referenced therein 
You make this selection during the initial scoping phase.
review each of Important to Know points referenced in documentation

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One 10.0 New Installation
entry.content:
Hi there.

We are not going to perform an upgrade, we want to install version 10.0 as a fresh installation and then move backups and update those backups.

The question is whether you can install the latest patch directly for a new installation or you have to start from a specific version or installation.

For example, in version 9.3 you had to start from the 9.3 SP00 PL05 DVD, which was the installation version, and then apply the appropriate patches.

entry.content: no

entry.title: Re: EVOLUTION OF STOCK QUERY
entry.content: Thanks Loha

entry.title: Re: ATCUD para Portugal: Prepare-se para o novo requisito legal da Portaria 195/2020
entry.content:
Hi Martha,

We have a issue with GLO_PT_SVC. After testing End to End scenarios in Quality system we moved changes to Production. Surprisingly after 1 st Invoice generation the row belonging to series identifier got deleted in GLO_PT_SVC because of which we are getting ATCUD code as 0 in our Invoice. Entries for this Series identifier/invoice type exists in SIPT_NUMBR_SD_V and SIPT_NUMBR_SD_V2 but not in GLO_PT_SVC.

We have already raised incident to SAP. Do you have any idea what may be the cause for deletion of ATCUD code row from  GLO_PT_SVC.

Thanks & Regards,

Divya

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2019.001 v1.62 novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content:
Durante a implementação da SAPnote 3423952 estou tendo o erro:

Include LJ1BBTOP
The field "IDNFBRJ_1BNFPRSMD_CRDT" is unknown, but there is a field
with the similar name "XJ_1BNFPRSMD_CRDT". "XJ_1BNFPRSMD_CRDT".

Contudo, não encontrei a Nota que cria o objeto desconhecido, alguem passou por isso?

Att Eduardo

entry.title: Re: SAP Security audit logs
entry.content: If you save the log events on the BD, then you could develop a ABAP program to export them on the format you need.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - build service not working since 24 hours
entry.content:
I'm still getting an error trying to build android:

Runtime version: 4.9.218; AB version: 2.1.14COMMAND:
NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16000  npxrnv export -p android -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.9.218 -r
FAILED with ERROR:
Command failed with exit code 1: npx rnv export -p android -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.9.218 -r

entry.title: BOM and Standard Cost
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently undergoing a big project which requires a query to display a BOM and its contents in the aim of a predictive std cost report. some of the fields i have left as NULL as this is down to manual input but whilst the query works to a degree i am unable to see when in BOMS another BOM has been used as a component:
this is my logic:

WITH ComponentPrices AS (
SELECT
T0.Father AS ParentItem,
T0.Code AS Component,
T0.Quantity,
T0.Price AS ComponentPrice,
T1.LstEvlPric AS STDComponentPrice,
T0.Quantity * T0.Price AS ComponentTotalPrice,
T0.Quantity * T1.LstEvlPric AS STDComponentTotalPrice
FROM ITT1 T0
INNER JOIN OITM T1 ON T0.Code = T1.ItemCode
WHERE T1.QryGroup6 = 'Y'
)

SELECT
T0.Father AS "PARENT PRODUCT CODE",
T0.VisOrder AS "Line Level",
T0.Code AS "PRODUCT COMPONENT",
T1.ItemName AS "COMPONENT DESCRIPTION",
T0.Quantity AS "QTY",
T0.Currency,
T0.Price AS "UNIT COST",
T0.Quantity * T0.Price AS "TOTAL COST",
T2.CardName AS "SUPPLIER",
T0.Quantity * T0.Price * 0.01 AS "HANDLING 1% (TBC)", -- Holding Charge - 1% of stock Price
T0.Quantity * T0.Price * 0.02 AS "CUSTOM 2% (TBC)",
'1.5' AS "TRANSPORT COST (%)",
T0.Quantity * T0.Price * 0.015 AS "TRANS COST (TBC)",
NULL AS "FLIGHT RISK(%)",
SUM(T0.Quantity * T1.LstEvlPric) * (SELECT TOP 1 Rate FROM ORTT WHERE Currency = 'EUR' AND RateDate <= CONVERT(DATE, GETDATE()) ORDER BY RateDate DESC) AS "STD 2024 IN €",
(SUM(T0.Quantity * T0.Price) + (T0.Quantity * T0.Price * 0.01) + (T0.Quantity * T0.Price * 0.02) + (T0.Quantity * T0.Price * 0.015)) AS "STD 2024 IN GBP",
NULL AS "STD 2023 IN €",
NULL AS "STD 2023 IN GBP",
NULL AS "DIFF **bleep** 24/23",
NULL AS "DIFF. % 24/23",
NULL AS "LIST",
'GBP' AS "CURRENCY",
NULL AS "LIST IN EURO",
NULL AS "COST EURO (X QNT)",
NULL AS "NOTE",
NULL AS "CODICE PRODOTTO",
NULL AS "TOTALE 2020 EURO"
FROM ITT1 T0
INNER JOIN OITM T1 ON T0.Code = T1.ItemCode
INNER JOIN OCRD T2 ON T1.CardCode = T2.CardCode
WHERE T1.QryGroup6 = 'Y'
GROUP BY T0.Father, T0.VisOrder, T0.Code, T1.ItemName, T0.Quantity, T0.Currency, T1.LstEvlPric, T2.CardName,T0.Price

UNION ALL

SELECT
ParentItem AS "PARENT PRODUCT CODE",
NULL AS "Line Level",
NULL AS "PRODUCT COMPONENT",
NULL AS "COMPONENT DESCRIPTION",
NULL AS "QTY",
NULL AS Currency,
MAX(ComponentPrice) AS "UNIT COST",
SUM(ComponentTotalPrice) AS "TOTAL COST",
NULL AS "SUPPLIER",
NULL AS "HDLN",
NULL AS "CUSTOM%",
NULL AS "TRASP. FOB (%)",
NULL AS "TRASP. FOB (euro)",
NULL AS "FLIGHT RISK(%)",
NULL AS "STD 2024 IN €",
NULL AS "STD 2024 IN GBP",
NULL AS "STD 2023 IN €",
NULL AS "STD 2023 IN GBP",
NULL AS "DIFF **bleep** 24/23",
NULL AS "DIFF. % 24/23",
NULL AS "LIST",
'GBP' AS "CURRENCY",
NULL AS "LIST IN EURO",
NULL AS "COST EURO (X QNT)",
NULL AS "NOTE",
NULL AS "CODICE PRODOTTO",
NULL AS "TOTALE 2020 EURO"
FROM ComponentPrices
GROUP BY ParentItem

ORDER BY "PARENT PRODUCT CODE", "Line Level"

 

Any assistance would be greatly appreciated.

 

Best wishes

Brad

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content:
Hi Stephanie,

Good Day !!!

I dont have suffiecient permission to create the blog ..what is the process and steps to be followed before I start or create the blogs.

 

Thanks

Harikrishna

entry.title: CI-DS Transport without dataintegration
entry.content:
Hello, 

I create a ci-ds job and I can see  that the query "MapToTarget" has both input data and output data. But if I check the dataintegration tile in IBP I get shown that there are no datas to integrate. 

I chekct the target, I chekct the global variable and I compare this job with another working job. I cannot see a difference.

I hope you can help me 

 

Regards 

Oliver

entry.title: Re: Unable to download in DMS using CAPM NodeJS
entry.content:
Hi @cdan,

Are you using DMS Integration Option? 

If so, you can check what is the correct syntax by using the CMIS Workbench application:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/utilizing-cmis-workbench-together-with-btp-dms/ba-p/13538866

Then, you will be able to "check" what is the correct syntax and path to reach your document - which can then be translated to a CAP service call.

If not, please let us know which API you are in fact using.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: How to use SAP MDG DQM (Data Quality Management) Validation Rules on Org Structure Units?
entry.content:
Dear SAP MDG Data Quality Management experts,

Is there a way to use SAP MDG DQM Validation Rules to ensure mandatory addition of an organisational unit (such as Distribution Chain / Sales Organization) in a Change Request? I need to prevent the users from forgetting to add at least 1 sales organisation / Distribution Chain. I've tried setting an expression condition to Sales Organisation is NOT initial but this didn't work. It looks as if DQM validations applicable to the fields belonging to an org structure unit are only executed once this unit itself is added. 

Thanks advance for your help

entry.title: Re: Posting date from EWM to EPR for inbound delivery
entry.content: What did not work for you?
